title: Chelonistele ingloria

taxonomy: Chelonistele ingloria is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, and its classification falls under the family Orchidaceae. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Chelonistele ingloria being a part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The subfamily Epidendroideae is a large and diverse group, comprising over 15,000 species of orchids. Chelonistele ingloria itself is a member of the genus Chelonistele, which is a small genus of terrestrial orchids. The exact classification of Chelonistele ingloria may vary depending on the source and the latest taxonomic research.
uses: Chelonistele ingloria, although not extensively documented, has been used in traditional medicine. The plant's parts are believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Its medicinal uses are primarily based on anecdotal evidence and require scientific validation.
